    efdtc_nodes ¶

    Classes:

    • BaseEFDTBranch –

      Node that splits the data in a EFDT.

    • BaseEFDTLeaf –

      Helper class that define basic operations of EFDT's nodes.

    • EFDTLeafMajorityClass –

      Active Learning node for the Hoeffding Anytime Tree.

    • EFDTLeafNaiveBayes –

      Learning node for the Hoeffding Anytime Tree that uses Naive Bayes

    • EFDTLeafNaiveBayesAdaptive –

      Learning node for the Hoeffding Anytime Tree that uses Adaptive Naive

    BaseEFDTBranch ¶

    BaseEFDTBranch(
    stats, *children, splitter, splitters, **attributes
)

    Bases: DTBranch

    
              flowchart TD
              vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.efdtc_nodes.BaseEFDTBranch[BaseEFDTBranch]
              vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.branch.DTBranch[DTBranch]
              vendor.river.river.tree.base.Branch[Branch]

                              vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.branch.DTBranch --> vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.efdtc_nodes.BaseEFDTBranch
                                vendor.river.river.tree.base.Branch --> vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.branch.DTBranch
                



              click vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.efdtc_nodes.BaseEFDTBranch href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.efdtc_nodes.BaseEFDTBranch"
              click vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.branch.DTBranch href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.branch.DTBranch"
              click vendor.river.river.tree.base.Branch href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.base.Branch"

    Node that splits the data in a EFDT.

    This node is an exception among the tree's nodes. EFDTSplitNode is both a split node and a learning node. EFDT updates all of the nodes in the path from the root to a leaf when a new instance arrives. Besides that, it also revisit split decisions from time to time. For that reason, this decision node also needs to be able to learn from new instances.

    Parameters¶

    stats Class observations children The children nodes. attributes Other parameters passed to the learning nodes.

    Methods:

    • best_split_suggestions –

      Find possible split candidates without taking into account the

    • find_attribute –

      Find the attribute given the id.

    • iter_bfs –

      Iterate over nodes in breadth-first order.

    • iter_branches –

      Iterate over branches in depth-first order.

    • iter_dfs –

      Iterate over nodes in depth-first order.

    • iter_edges –

      Iterate over edges in depth-first order.

    • iter_leaves –

      Iterate over leaves from the left-most one to the right-most one.

    • learn_one –

      Update branch with the provided sample.

    • most_common_path –

      Return a tuple with the branch index and the child node related to the most

    • observed_class_distribution_is_pure –

      Check if observed class distribution is pure, i.e. if all samples

    • repr_branch –

      Return a string representation of the test performed in the branch at index.

    • to_dataframe –

      Build a DataFrame containing one record for each node.

    • traverse –

      Return the leaf corresponding to the given input.

    • walk –

      Iterate over the nodes of the path induced by x.

    Attributes:

    • height –

      Distance to the deepest descendant.

    • last_split_reevaluation_at (float) –

      Get the weight seen at the last split reevaluation.

    • n_branches –

      Number of branches, including thyself.

    • n_leaves –

      Number of leaves.

    • n_nodes –

      Number of descendants, including thyself.

    • repr_split –

      String representation of the split.

    height property ¶

    height

    Distance to the deepest descendant.

    last_split_reevaluation_at property writable ¶

    last_split_reevaluation_at: float

    Get the weight seen at the last split reevaluation.

    Returns¶
    Total weight seen at last split reevaluation.

    n_branches property ¶

    n_branches

    Number of branches, including thyself.

    n_leaves property ¶

    n_leaves

    Number of leaves.

    n_nodes property ¶

    n_nodes

    Number of descendants, including thyself.

    repr_split abstractmethod property ¶

    repr_split

    String representation of the split.

    best_split_suggestions ¶

    best_split_suggestions(criterion, tree)

    Find possible split candidates without taking into account the null split.

    Parameters¶

    criterion The splitting criterion to be used. tree The EFDT which the node belongs to.

    Returns¶
    The list of split candidates.

    find_attribute staticmethod ¶

    find_attribute(id_att, split_suggestions)

    Find the attribute given the id.

    Parameters¶

    id_att Id of attribute to find. split_suggestions Possible split candidates. Returns

    Found attribute.

    iter_bfs ¶

    iter_bfs()

    Iterate over nodes in breadth-first order.

    iter_branches ¶

    iter_branches()

    Iterate over branches in depth-first order.

    iter_dfs ¶

    iter_dfs()

    Iterate over nodes in depth-first order.

    iter_edges ¶

    iter_edges()

    Iterate over edges in depth-first order.

    iter_leaves ¶

    iter_leaves()

    Iterate over leaves from the left-most one to the right-most one.

    learn_one ¶

    learn_one(x, y, *, w=1.0, tree=None)

    Update branch with the provided sample.

    Parameters¶

    x Sample attributes for updating the node. y Target value. w Sample weight. tree Tree to update.

    most_common_path abstractmethod ¶

    most_common_path() -> tuple[int, Leaf | Branch]

    Return a tuple with the branch index and the child node related to the most traversed path.

    Used in case the split feature is missing from an instance.

    observed_class_distribution_is_pure ¶

    observed_class_distribution_is_pure()

    Check if observed class distribution is pure, i.e. if all samples belong to the same class.

    Returns¶
    True if the observed number of classes is smaller than 2, False otherwise.

    repr_branch abstractmethod ¶

    repr_branch(index: int, shorten=False)

    Return a string representation of the test performed in the branch at index.

    Parameters¶

    index The branch index. shorten If True, return a shortened version of the performed test.

    to_dataframe ¶

    to_dataframe() -> DataFrame

    Build a DataFrame containing one record for each node.

    traverse ¶

    traverse(x, until_leaf=True) -> Branch | Leaf

    Return the leaf corresponding to the given input.

    walk ¶

    walk(x, until_leaf=True) -> Iterable[Branch | Leaf]

    Iterate over the nodes of the path induced by x.

    BaseEFDTLeaf ¶

    BaseEFDTLeaf(stats, depth, splitter, **kwargs)

    Bases: HTLeaf

    
              flowchart TD
              vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.efdtc_nodes.BaseEFDTLeaf[BaseEFDTLeaf]
              vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.leaf.HTLeaf[HTLeaf]
              vendor.river.river.tree.base.Leaf[Leaf]

                              vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.leaf.HTLeaf --> vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.efdtc_nodes.BaseEFDTLeaf
                                vendor.river.river.tree.base.Leaf --> vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.leaf.HTLeaf
                



              click vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.efdtc_nodes.BaseEFDTLeaf href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.efdtc_nodes.BaseEFDTLeaf"
              click vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.leaf.HTLeaf href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.leaf.HTLeaf"
              click vendor.river.river.tree.base.Leaf href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.base.Leaf"

    Helper class that define basic operations of EFDT's nodes.

    It inherits from LearningNode and provides extra functionalities, while changing the splitting behavior of its parent class. This is an abstract class, since it does not implement all the inherited abstract methods from its parent class. BaseEDFTNode is designed to work with other learning/split nodes.

    Parameters¶

    stats Class observations. depth The depth of the node in the tree. splitter The numeric attribute observer algorithm used to monitor target statistics and perform split attempts. kwargs Other parameters passed to the node.

    Methods:

    • best_split_suggestions –

      Find possible split candidates without taking into account the

    • calculate_promise –

      Calculate node's promise.

    • disable_attribute –

      Disable an attribute observer.

    • learn_one –

      Update the node with the provided sample.

    Attributes:

    • last_split_attempt_at (float) –

      The weight seen at last split evaluation.

    last_split_attempt_at property writable ¶

    last_split_attempt_at: float

    The weight seen at last split evaluation.

    Returns¶

    Weight seen at last split evaluation.

    best_split_suggestions ¶

    best_split_suggestions(criterion, tree)

    Find possible split candidates without taking into account the null split.

    Parameters¶

    criterion The splitting criterion to be used. tree The EFDT which the node belongs to.

    Returns¶
    The list of split candidates.

    calculate_promise abstractmethod ¶

    calculate_promise() -> int

    Calculate node's promise.

    Returns¶

    int A small value indicates that the node has seen more samples of a given class than the other classes.

    disable_attribute ¶

    disable_attribute(att_id)

    Disable an attribute observer.

    Parameters¶

    att_id Attribute index.

    learn_one ¶

    learn_one(x, y, *, w=1.0, tree=None)

    Update the node with the provided sample.

    Parameters¶

    x Sample attributes for updating the node. y Target value. w Sample weight. tree Tree to update.

    Notes¶

    This base implementation defines the basic functioning of a learning node. All classes overriding this method should include a call to super().learn_one to guarantee the learning process happens consistently.

              click vendor.river.river.tree.base.Leaf href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.base.Leaf"

    Active Learning node for the Hoeffding Anytime Tree.

    Parameters¶

    stats Initial class observations. depth The depth of the node. splitter The numeric attribute observer algorithm used to monitor target statistics and perform split attempts. kwargs Other parameters passed to the learning nodes.

    Methods:

    • best_split_suggestions –

      Find possible split candidates without taking into account the

    • calculate_promise –

      Calculate how likely a node is going to be split.

    • disable_attribute –

      Disable an attribute observer.

    • learn_one –

      Update the node with the provided sample.

    • observed_class_distribution_is_pure –

      Check if observed class distribution is pure, i.e. if all samples

    Attributes:

    • last_split_attempt_at (float) –

      The weight seen at last split evaluation.

    • total_weight –

      Calculate the total weight seen by the node.

    last_split_attempt_at property writable ¶

    last_split_attempt_at: float

    The weight seen at last split evaluation.

    Returns¶

    Weight seen at last split evaluation.

    total_weight property ¶

    total_weight

    Calculate the total weight seen by the node.

    Returns¶
    Total weight seen.

    best_split_suggestions ¶

    best_split_suggestions(criterion, tree)

    Find possible split candidates without taking into account the null split.

    Parameters¶

    criterion The splitting criterion to be used. tree The EFDT which the node belongs to.

    Returns¶
    The list of split candidates.

    calculate_promise ¶

    calculate_promise()

    Calculate how likely a node is going to be split.

    A node with a (close to) pure class distribution will less likely be split.

    Returns¶
    A small value indicates that the node has seen more samples of a
given class than the other classes.

    disable_attribute ¶

    disable_attribute(att_id)

    Disable an attribute observer.

    Parameters¶

    att_id Attribute index.

    learn_one ¶

    learn_one(x, y, *, w=1.0, tree=None)

    Update the node with the provided sample.

    Parameters¶

    x Sample attributes for updating the node. y Target value. w Sample weight. tree Tree to update.

    Notes¶

    This base implementation defines the basic functioning of a learning node. All classes overriding this method should include a call to super().learn_one to guarantee the learning process happens consistently.

    observed_class_distribution_is_pure ¶

    observed_class_distribution_is_pure()

    Check if observed class distribution is pure, i.e. if all samples belong to the same class.

    Returns¶
    True if observed number of classes is less than 2, False otherwise.

    EFDTLeafNaiveBayes ¶

    EFDTLeafNaiveBayes(stats, depth, splitter, **kwargs)

    Bases: BaseEFDTLeaf, LeafNaiveBayes

              click vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.efdtc_nodes.EFDTLeafNaiveBayes href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.efdtc_nodes.EFDTLeafNaiveBayes"
              click vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.efdtc_nodes.BaseEFDTLeaf href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.efdtc_nodes.BaseEFDTLeaf"
              click vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.htc_nodes.LeafNaiveBayes href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.htc_nodes.LeafNaiveBayes"
              click vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.htc_nodes.LeafMajorityClass href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.htc_nodes.LeafMajorityClass"
              click vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.leaf.HTLeaf href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.leaf.HTLeaf"
              click vendor.river.river.tree.base.Leaf href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.base.Leaf"

    Learning node for the Hoeffding Anytime Tree that uses Naive Bayes models.

    Parameters¶

    stats Initial class observations depth The depth of the node. splitter The numeric attribute observer algorithm used to monitor target statistics and perform split attempts. kwargs Other parameters passed to the learning nodes.

    Methods:

    • best_split_suggestions –

      Find possible split candidates without taking into account the

    • calculate_promise –

      Calculate how likely a node is going to be split.

    • disable_attribute –

      Disable an attribute observer.

    • learn_one –

      Update the node with the provided sample.

    • observed_class_distribution_is_pure –

      Check if observed class distribution is pure, i.e. if all samples

    Attributes:

    • last_split_attempt_at (float) –

      The weight seen at last split evaluation.

    • total_weight –

      Calculate the total weight seen by the node.

    last_split_attempt_at property writable ¶

    last_split_attempt_at: float

    The weight seen at last split evaluation.

    Returns¶

    Weight seen at last split evaluation.

    total_weight property ¶

    total_weight

    Calculate the total weight seen by the node.

    Returns¶
    Total weight seen.

    best_split_suggestions ¶

    best_split_suggestions(criterion, tree)

    Find possible split candidates without taking into account the null split.

    Parameters¶

    criterion The splitting criterion to be used. tree The EFDT which the node belongs to.

    Returns¶
    The list of split candidates.

    calculate_promise ¶

    calculate_promise()

    Calculate how likely a node is going to be split.

    A node with a (close to) pure class distribution will less likely be split.

    Returns¶
    A small value indicates that the node has seen more samples of a
given class than the other classes.

    disable_attribute ¶

    disable_attribute(att_index)

    Disable an attribute observer.

    Disabled in Nodes using Naive Bayes, since poor attributes are used in Naive Bayes calculation.

    Parameters¶

    att_index Attribute index.

    learn_one ¶

    learn_one(x, y, *, w=1.0, tree=None)

    Update the node with the provided sample.

    Parameters¶

    x Sample attributes for updating the node. y Target value. w Sample weight. tree Tree to update.

    Notes¶

    This base implementation defines the basic functioning of a learning node. All classes overriding this method should include a call to super().learn_one to guarantee the learning process happens consistently.

    observed_class_distribution_is_pure ¶

    observed_class_distribution_is_pure()

    Check if observed class distribution is pure, i.e. if all samples belong to the same class.

    Returns¶
    True if observed number of classes is less than 2, False otherwise.

)

                                vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.htc_nodes.LeafMajorityClass --> vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.htc_nodes.LeafNaiveBayesAdaptive
                                vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.leaf.HTLeaf --> vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.htc_nodes.LeafMajorityClass
                                vendor.river.river.tree.base.Leaf --> vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.leaf.HTLeaf
                





              click vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.efdtc_nodes.EFDTLeafNaiveBayesAdaptive href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.efdtc_nodes.EFDTLeafNaiveBayesAdaptive"
              click vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.efdtc_nodes.BaseEFDTLeaf href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.efdtc_nodes.BaseEFDTLeaf"
              click vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.htc_nodes.LeafNaiveBayesAdaptive href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.htc_nodes.LeafNaiveBayesAdaptive"
              click vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.htc_nodes.LeafMajorityClass href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.htc_nodes.LeafMajorityClass"
              click vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.leaf.HTLeaf href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.nodes.leaf.HTLeaf"
              click vendor.river.river.tree.base.Leaf href "" "vendor.river.river.tree.base.Leaf"

    Learning node for the Hoeffding Anytime Tree that uses Adaptive Naive Bayes models.

    Parameters¶

    stats Initial class observations. depth The depth of the node. splitter The numeric attribute observer algorithm used to monitor target statistics and perform split attempts. kwargs Other parameters passed to the learning nodes.

    Methods:

    • best_split_suggestions –

      Find possible split candidates without taking into account the

    • calculate_promise –

      Calculate how likely a node is going to be split.

    • disable_attribute –

      Disable an attribute observer.

    • learn_one –

      Update the node with the provided instance.

    • observed_class_distribution_is_pure –

      Check if observed class distribution is pure, i.e. if all samples

    • prediction –

      Get the probabilities per class for a given instance.

    Attributes:

    • last_split_attempt_at (float) –

      The weight seen at last split evaluation.

    • total_weight –

      Calculate the total weight seen by the node.

    last_split_attempt_at property writable ¶

    last_split_attempt_at: float

    The weight seen at last split evaluation.

    Returns¶

    Weight seen at last split evaluation.

    total_weight property ¶

    total_weight

    Calculate the total weight seen by the node.

    Returns¶
    Total weight seen.

    best_split_suggestions ¶

    best_split_suggestions(criterion, tree)

    Find possible split candidates without taking into account the null split.

    Parameters¶

    criterion The splitting criterion to be used. tree The EFDT which the node belongs to.

    Returns¶
    The list of split candidates.

    calculate_promise ¶

    calculate_promise()

    Calculate how likely a node is going to be split.

    A node with a (close to) pure class distribution will less likely be split.

    Returns¶
    A small value indicates that the node has seen more samples of a
given class than the other classes.

    disable_attribute ¶

    disable_attribute(att_index)

    Disable an attribute observer.

    Disabled in Nodes using Naive Bayes, since poor attributes are used in Naive Bayes calculation.

    Parameters¶

    att_index Attribute index.

    learn_one ¶

    learn_one(x, y, *, w=1.0, tree=None)

    Update the node with the provided instance.

    Parameters¶

    x Instance attributes for updating the node. y Instance class. w The instance's weight. tree The Hoeffding Tree to update.

    observed_class_distribution_is_pure ¶

    observed_class_distribution_is_pure()

    Check if observed class distribution is pure, i.e. if all samples belong to the same class.

    Returns¶
    True if observed number of classes is less than 2, False otherwise.

    prediction ¶

    prediction(x, *, tree=None)

    Get the probabilities per class for a given instance.

    Parameters¶

    x Instance attributes. tree Hoeffding Tree.

    Returns¶

    Class votes for the given instance.

    Previous
    branch
    Next
    et_nodes
    Made with Material for MkDocs
